Claude Team.sentiment_summary: Adopted as default LLM stack at small PE firms building AI-first infrastructure. Used as the foundation for NDA review, memo drafting, modeling support.
common_praise: team plan unlocks Claude Projects which give context isolation per workflow

example_quotes.author: u/Strong-Buy-6466
example_quotes.snippet: tasked with building / setting up an AI-first infrastructure using Claude (Team plan), with the goal of automating as much as possible across our workflow

ChatGPT (chat vs API).sentiment_summary: Heavy gap between chat UX and API behavior. Chat has retrieval, system prompts, and guardrails baked in; teams who try to rebuild a production workflow on the API underestimate the rebuild cost by months.
common_complaints: chat interface flatters you — feels capable, but the API drops all the scaffolding
production workflow on raw API takes way longer than the chat demo suggests

example_quotes.author: u/Away-Lengthiness-831
example_quotes.snippet: ChatGPT in the chat interface is not the same as ChatGPT through the API when you are trying to run a real production workflow

example_quotes.author: u/NecessaryPapaya51
example_quotes.snippet: The ChatGPT chat vs API thing is one of the most common traps right now... When you go to build it for real, you're essentially rebuilding all of that yourself

Docling.sentiment_summary: Recommended as a parsing/OCR layer for messy LP documents before any LLM step. The community view: extraction is the bottleneck, not the LLM.
common_praise: good for structural parsing of PDFs

example_quotes.author: u/NecessaryPapaya51
example_quotes.snippet: Look at Docling, Unstructured.io, or AWS Textract for the OCR and structural piece

Unstructured.io.sentiment_summary: Same use case as Docling — parse PDFs into clean structured text before LLM normalization.

example_quotes.author: u/NecessaryPapaya51
example_quotes.snippet: Look at Docling, Unstructured.io, or AWS Textract

AWS Textract.sentiment_summary: Enterprise-friendly OCR option. Mentioned alongside Docling and Unstructured.io as the parsing layer that should sit before the LLM.

example_quotes.author: u/NecessaryPapaya51
example_quotes.snippet: Look at Docling, Unstructured.io, or AWS Textract for the OCR and structural piece

Preqin.sentiment_summary: Standard PE peer-fund benchmarking source. Coverage is weak for smaller funds — consistent complaint across firms doing in-house benchmarking. Useful as a validation layer (cross-check) even when coverage gaps force in-house builds.
common_complaints: coverage gaps force firms to build in-house
common_praise: good as a cross-check / validation layer for funds it does cover

example_quotes.author: u/Away-Lengthiness-831
example_quotes.snippet: We do have access to Preqin and Bloomberg, but we kept finding that the data is often outdated, especially for smaller funds

Bloomberg.sentiment_summary: Same role as Preqin in PE benchmarking. Same coverage gaps for smaller funds.

example_quotes.author: u/Away-Lengthiness-831
example_quotes.snippet: We do have access to Preqin and Bloomberg, but we kept finding that the data is often outdated

Perplexity.sentiment_summary: Used alongside Claude for data extraction with confidence scoring. Some users have churned the subscription.
common_complaints: enough overlap with Claude that some users drop it
common_praise: confidence % output helps validate extracted data

example_quotes.author: u/thepookienextdoor
example_quotes.snippet: since I've been using Claude and perplexity, adding a layer of confidence % in data extraction is what has helped me
